featuretablefilter 0.99.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • featuretablefilter Tutorial: Complete Guide to Microbiome Feature Table Filtering
  • Changelog

Generic conversion function for feature tables

Source: R/convert_data_classes.R
convert_feature_table.Rd

Automatically detects the input class and converts to the appropriate format. Supports phyloseq, TreeSummarizedExperiment, SingleCellExperiment, and data.frame inputs.

convert_feature_table(x, to = c("data.frame", "phyloseq", "TSE"), ...)

Arguments

x

Object to convert (phyloseq, TreeSummarizedExperiment, SingleCellExperiment, or data.frame)

to

Target class: "data.frame", "phyloseq", or "TSE" (TreeSummarizedExperiment)

...

Additional arguments passed to the specific conversion function

Value

Converted object in the target format

Examples

data(example_feature_table)
df <- convert_feature_table(example_feature_table, to = "data.frame")
head(df)
#>   #OTU ID Sample_001 Sample_002 Sample_003 Sample_004 Sample_005 Sample_006
#> 1 ASV_001       1245        892       1567        734       1123       1456
#> 2 ASV_002        567        423        689        312        534        712
#> 3 ASV_003        234        178        289        123        201        267
#> 4 ASV_004         89         67        112         45         78         98
#> 5 ASV_005       3456       2789       4123       2156       3234       3987
#> 6 ASV_006      12345      10234      14567       8765      11234      13456
#>   Sample_007 Sample_008 Sample_009 Sample_010 Sample_011 Sample_012 Sample_013
#> 1        987       1234        876       1345       1098        765       1432
#> 2        456        598        387        623        498        345        678
#> 3        156        223        134        245        189        112        256
#> 4         56         82         48         91         71         38         95
#> 5       2678       3456       2345       3678       3012       2234       3890
#> 6       9876      12123       9234      12789      10987       8456      13890
#>   Sample_014 Sample_015 Sample_016 Sample_017 Sample_018 Sample_019 Sample_020
#> 1        923       1156       1287        845       1376       1021       1198
#> 2        412        545        601        378        645        467        523
#> 3        145        198        234        128        267        167        212
#> 4         52         73         87         42         99         61         79
#> 5       2567       3123       3567       2456       3789       2890       3345
#> 6       9567      11567      12890       8976      13234      10456      11890
